title: Delicate Snappy Korean Herbs Hwajeon
description: A traditional Korean dish featuring a delicate and snappy texture, made with herbal ingredients and a hint of vegan white wine vinegar, perfect for the monsoon season. This dish is vegan-friendly and cooked to perfection in a cast-iron skillet.

ingredients.name: Korean herbs
ingredients.notes: such as mugwort, sesame leaves, and garlic chives

instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the Korean chili flakes and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the vegan white wine vinegar, water, and Korean herbs, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the rice flour and salt.
Add the crumbled tofu to the bowl and mix well.
Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the tofu mixture into the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy.
Repeat with the remaining tofu mixture, adjusting the heat as needed to prevent burning.
Serve the hwajeon hot, garnished with additional Korean herbs if desired.
Deglaze the skillet with a small amount of vegan white wine vinegar to release the fond and serve as a sauce.
